journal_entry.reason: Record the refinancing of the old bank loan with a new bank loan. The old note payable is removed from the books (debit) and the new note payable is recorded (credit) at the same principal amount of $100,000.

output_debit_classifier.reason: This is a refinancing transaction where an existing bank loan is replaced with a new bank loan of equal principal amount. Under the conventional term 'refinanced', the old obligation is extinguished (debit to remove the old liability) and a new obligation is created (credit to record the new liability). The amounts are equal at $100,000, so debits equal credits. No cash changes hands in a pure refinancing, and no transaction costs are mentioned, so no additional accounts are needed.
